title: Introduction to Computing
programName: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
description: This course provides an overview of the computing field and its fundamental concepts. Topics include computer hardware, software, networks, and the impact of computers on society.

title: Computer Programming 1
programName: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
description: Introduction to programming concepts using a modern programming language. Covers variables, control structures, functions, and basic data structures.

title: Computer Programming 2
programName: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
description: Advanced programming concepts including object-oriented programming, file handling, and basic data structures.

title: Data Structures and Algorithms
programName: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
description: Study of fundamental data structures including stacks, queues, trees, and graphs. Algorithm analysis and design techniques.

title: Database Management System
programName: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
description: Introduction to database concepts, design, and implementation. SQL programming and database administration basics.

title: Introduction to Computer Science
programName: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
description: Foundational concepts of computer science including algorithms, complexity, and theoretical models of computation.

title: Programming Fundamentals
programName: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
description: Introduction to programming principles and practices using a modern programming language.

title: Object-Oriented Programming
programName: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
description: Advanced programming concepts focusing on object-oriented design patterns and principles.

title: Computer Fundamentals
description: Basic concepts of computer hardware, software, and operating systems.

title: Advanced Algorithms
programName: Master of Science in Computer Science
description: Advanced study of algorithm design, analysis, and complexity theory.
